\# Vendor Repository Index v0.1



Artifact: Vendor Repository Index

Version: 0.1

Status: Active

Purpose: Record vendor repositories that may inform Governance Field Computing without granting them automatic authority.

Depends On: Vendor Repository Intake Standard v0.1

Required By: Future vendor scans, concept extraction, source comparison, and GFC integration decisions.

Evidence Level: Repository inventory

Date: 2026-06-23



\---



\## 1. Core Rule



This index records vendor repositories only.



Inclusion in this index does not mean that repository content is accepted into Governance Field Computing.



Vendor repositories are reference sources.



Governance Field Computing remains the controlling repository for all GFC definitions, schemas, validators, decision records, and methodology.



\---



\## 2. Vendor Repository Table



| ID | Repository | Visibility | Current Role | Intake Status | Import Status |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| VENDOR-001 | kevinlrankin-ux/Context-Collapsed-Key-Management-CCKM | Private | Candidate source for context collapse, key management, and token/meaning boundary concepts | Indexed | Not Imported |

| VENDOR-002 | kevinlrankin-ux/ccbp-governance-adapter | Public | Candidate source for governance adapter patterns and boundary-aware interface logic | Indexed | Not Imported |

| VENDOR-003 | kevinlrankin-ux/constitutional-language-protocol | Public | Candidate source for constitutional language constraints, terminology rules, and formal language discipline | Indexed | Not Imported |

| VENDOR-004 | kevinlrankin-ux/consciousness-capability-boundary-project | Public | Candidate source for capability boundary logic, constitutional framing, and decision discipline | Indexed | Not Imported |

| VENDOR-005 | kevinlrankin-ux/-legitimacy-and-warrant-sandbox | Private | Candidate source for legitimacy, warrant, evidence, bounded standing, review routing, and drift feedback | Indexed | Not Imported |

| VENDOR-006 | kevinlrankin-ux/-adaptive-learning-systems-profile-proof | Private | Candidate source for adaptive learning, profile proof, learning-state verification, and evidence-bearing progression models | Indexed | Not Imported |



\---



\## 3. Vendor Intake Status Definitions



\### Indexed



The repository has been recorded as a vendor source.



\### Reviewed



The repository has been inspected at the README or manifest level.



\### Candidate Extracted



A specific concept, schema, or test strategy has been identified for possible GFC use.



\### Accepted



A specific vendor-derived concept has been formally accepted by GFC decision record.



\### Rejected



A specific vendor-derived concept has been formally rejected by GFC decision record.



\### Deferred



A specific vendor-derived concept has been postponed for later review.



\---



\## 4. Import Status Definitions



\### Not Imported



No vendor content has been incorporated into Governance Field Computing.



\### Reference Only



Vendor material may be cited or discussed, but not adopted.



\### Adapted



Vendor material has been modified and accepted into GFC through decision review.



\### Promoted



Vendor material has become part of GFC methodology, schema, or validator logic through decision review.



\---



\## 5. Initial Contribution Hypotheses



\### VENDOR-001 — Context-Collapsed-Key-Management-CCKM



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Context collapse

\- Keyed reconstruction

\- Sparse retrieval

\- Meaning-bearing tokens

\- Governance phrase as compressed pointer



Risk:



\- May contain project-specific assumptions not suitable for GFC.



Initial disposition:



Reference only until reviewed.



\---



\### VENDOR-002 — ccbp-governance-adapter



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Adapter logic

\- Constitutional boundary translation

\- Interface between abstract principles and executable behavior

\- Governance phrase routing



Risk:



\- May depend on CCBP-specific commitments that should not automatically migrate into GFC.



Initial disposition:



Reference only until reviewed.



\---



\### VENDOR-003 — constitutional-language-protocol



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Constitutional wording standards

\- Controlled language

\- Shall / shall not constraints

\- Drift prevention

\- Legal-semantic operator discipline



Risk:



\- May be highly relevant, but must not override GFC definitions without decision review.



Initial disposition:



High-priority review candidate.



\---



\### VENDOR-004 — consciousness-capability-boundary-project



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Capability boundary discipline

\- Constitutional framing

\- Non-attribution rules

\- Governance constraints

\- Decision architecture



Risk:



\- Broad scope may introduce abstraction drift if imported too freely.



Initial disposition:



Reference only until specific candidate concepts are identified.



\---



\### VENDOR-005 — legitimacy-and-warrant-sandbox



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Warrant structures

\- Legitimacy testing

\- Evidence status

\- Review discipline

\- Claim support hierarchy

\- Bounded standing

\- Domain-bounded legitimacy

\- Incident and drift feedback



Risk:



\- Sandbox status may indicate experimental or unstable content.

\- Warrant and legitimacy are adjacent to GFC semantic validation, but not identical to it.



Initial disposition:



High-priority review candidate for evidence, warrant, legitimacy, and semantic-validation concepts.



\---



\### VENDOR-006 — adaptive-learning-systems-profile-proof



Possible GFC relevance:



\- Adaptive learning systems

\- Profile proof

\- Evidence-bearing progression

\- Learning-state verification

\- Competence progression

\- Demonstrated capability over time

\- Possible models for proving whether a system has actually learned, adapted, or improved



Risk:



\- May introduce education-specific or learner-profile assumptions that should not automatically migrate into GFC.

\- Profile proof may be relevant to evidence and progression, but must not be confused with governance legitimacy unless reviewed.



Initial disposition:



Reference only until reviewed.



\---



\## 6. Current Review Priority



Current recommended review order:



1\. legitimacy-and-warrant-sandbox

2\. constitutional-language-protocol

3\. adaptive-learning-systems-profile-proof

4\. Context-Collapsed-Key-Management-CCKM

5\. ccbp-governance-adapter

6\. consciousness-capability-boundary-project



Reason:



Governance Field Computing is currently moving from structural validation toward semantic validation.



The most immediately relevant vendor materials are likely legitimacy/warrant models, constitutional language discipline, and proof/progression models.



\---



\## 7. Non-Import Reminder



No content from these repositories is accepted into Governance Field Computing by this index.



This file only records that the repositories exist as vendor sources and identifies possible review value.
